NKJV.1: My little children, these things I write to you, so that you may not sin. And if anyone sins, we have an Advocate with the Father, Jesus Christ the righteous.
NKJV.2: And He Himself is the propitiation for our sins, and not for ours only but also for the whole world.
NKJV.3: Now by this we know that we know Him, if we keep His commandments.
NKJV.4: He who says, “I know Him,” and does not keep His commandments, is a liar, and the truth is not in him.
NKJV.5: But whoever keeps His word, truly the love of God is perfected in him. By this we know that we are in Him.
NKJV.6: He who says he abides in Him ought himself also to walk just as He walked.
NKJV.7: Brethren, I write no new commandment to you, but an old commandment which you have had from the beginning. The old commandment is the word which you heard from the beginning.
NKJV.8: Again, a new commandment I write to you, which thing is true in Him and in you, because the darkness is passing away, and the true light is already shining.
NKJV.9: He who says he is in the light, and hates his brother, is in darkness until now.
NKJV.10: He who loves his brother abides in the light, and there is no cause for stumbling in him.
NKJV.11: But he who hates his brother is in darkness and walks in darkness, and does not know where he is going, because the darkness has blinded his eyes.
NKJV.12: I write to you, little children, Because your sins are forgiven you for His name’s sake.
NKJV.13: I write to you, fathers, Because you have known Him who is from the beginning. I write to you, young men, Because you have overcome the wicked one. I write to you, little children, Because you have known the Father.
NKJV.14: I have written to you, fathers, Because you have known Him who is from the beginning. I have written to you, young men, Because you are strong, and the word of God abides in you, And you have overcome the wicked one.
NKJV.15: Do not love the world or the things in the world. If anyone loves the world, the love of the Father is not in him.
NKJV.16: For all that is in the world—the lust of the flesh, the lust of the eyes, and the pride of life—is not of the Father but is of the world.
NKJV.17: And the world is passing away, and the lust of it; but he who does the will of God abides forever.
NKJV.18: Little children, it is the last hour; and as you have heard that the Antichrist is coming, even now many antichrists have come, by which we know that it is the last hour.
NKJV.19: They went out from us, but they were not of us; for if they had been of us, they would have continued with us; but they went out that they might be made manifest, that none of them were of us.
NKJV.20: But you have an anointing from the Holy One, and you know all things.
NKJV.21: I have not written to you because you do not know the truth, but because you know it, and that no lie is of the truth.
NKJV.22: Who is a liar but he who denies that Jesus is the Christ? He is antichrist who denies the Father and the Son.
NKJV.23: Whoever denies the Son does not have the Father either; he who acknowledges the Son has the Father also.
NKJV.24: Therefore let that abide in you which you heard from the beginning. If what you heard from the beginning abides in you, you also will abide in the Son and in the Father.
NKJV.25: And this is the promise that He has promised us—eternal life.
NKJV.26: These things I have written to you concerning those who try to deceive you.
NKJV.27: But the anointing which you have received from Him abides in you, and you do not need that anyone teach you; but as the same anointing teaches you concerning all things, and is true, and is not a lie, and just as it has taught you, you will abide in Him.
NKJV.28: And now, little children, abide in Him, that when He appears, we may have confidence and not be ashamed before Him at His coming.
NKJV.29: If you know that He is righteous, you know that everyone who practices righteousness is born of Him.

ESV.1: My little children, I am writing these things to you so that you may not sin. But if anyone does sin, we have an advocate with the Father, Jesus Christ the righteous.
ESV.2: He is the propitiation for our sins, and not for ours only but also for the sins of the whole world.
ESV.3: And by this we know that we have come to know him, if we keep his commandments.
ESV.4: Whoever says “I know him” but does not keep his commandments is a liar, and the truth is not in him,
ESV.5: but whoever keeps his word, in him truly the love of God is perfected. By this we may know that we are in him:
ESV.6: whoever says he abides in him ought to walk in the same way in which he walked.
ESV.7: Beloved, I am writing you no new commandment, but an old commandment that you had from the beginning. The old commandment is the word that you have heard.
ESV.8: At the same time, it is a new commandment that I am writing to you, which is true in him and in you, because  the darkness is passing away and the true light is already shining.
ESV.9: Whoever says he is in the light and hates his brother is still in darkness.
ESV.10: Whoever loves his brother abides in the light, and in him  there is no cause for stumbling.
ESV.11: But whoever hates his brother is in the darkness and walks in the darkness, and does not know where he is going, because the darkness has blinded his eyes.
ESV.12: I am writing to you, little children, because your sins are forgiven for his name's sake.
ESV.13: I am writing to you, fathers, because you know him who is from the beginning. I am writing to you, young men, because you have overcome the evil one. I write to you, children, because you know the Father.
ESV.14: I write to you, fathers, because you know him who is from the beginning. I write to you, young men, because you are strong, and the word of God abides in you, and you have overcome the evil one.
ESV.15: Do not love the world or the things in the world. If anyone loves the world, the love of the Father is not in him.
ESV.16: For all that is in the world — the desires of the flesh and the desires of the eyes and pride of life  — is not from the Father but is from the world.
ESV.17: And the world is passing away along with its desires, but whoever does the will of God abides forever.
ESV.18: Children, it is the last hour, and as you have heard that antichrist is coming, so now many antichrists have come. Therefore we know that it is the last hour.
ESV.19: They went out from us, but they were not of us; for if they had been of us, they would have continued with us. But they went out, that it might become plain that they all are not of us.
ESV.20: But you have been anointed by the Holy One, and you all have knowledge.
ESV.21: I write to you, not because you do not know the truth, but because you know it, and because no lie is of the truth.
ESV.22: Who is the liar but he who denies that Jesus is the Christ? This is the antichrist, he who denies the Father and the Son.
ESV.23: No one who denies the Son has the Father. Whoever confesses the Son has the Father also.
ESV.24: Let what you heard from the beginning abide in you. If what you heard from the beginning abides in you, then you too will abide in the Son and in the Father.
ESV.25: And this is the promise that he made to us  — eternal life.
ESV.26: I write these things to you about those who are trying to deceive you.
ESV.27: But the anointing that you received from him abides in you, and you have no need that anyone should teach you. But as his anointing teaches you about everything, and is true, and is no lie — just as it has taught you, abide in him.
ESV.28: And now, little children, abide in him, so that when he appears we may have confidence and not shrink from him in shame at his coming.
ESV.29: If you know that he is righteous, you may be sure that everyone who practices righteousness has been born of him.

NASB.1: My little children, I am writing these things to you so that you may not sin. And if anyone sins, we have an Advocate with the Father, Jesus Christ the righteous;
NASB.2: and He Himself is the propitiation for our sins; and not for ours only, but also for [those of] the whole world.
NASB.3: By this we know that we have come to know Him, if we keep His commandments.
NASB.4: The one who says, 'I have come to know Him,' and does not keep His commandments, is a liar, and the truth is not in him;
NASB.5: but whoever keeps His word, in him the love of God has truly been perfected. By this we know that we are in Him:
NASB.6: the one who says he abides in Him ought himself to walk in the same manner as He walked.
NASB.7: Beloved, I am not writing a new commandment to you, but an old commandment which you have had from the beginning; the old commandment is the word which you have heard.
NASB.8: On the other hand, I am writing a new commandment to you, which is true in Him and in you, because the darkness is passing away and the true Light is already shining.
NASB.9: The one who says he is in the Light and [yet] hates his brother is in the darkness until now.
NASB.10: The one who loves his brother abides in the Light and there is no cause for stumbling in him.
NASB.11: But the one who hates his brother is in the darkness and walks in the darkness, and does not know where he is going because the darkness has blinded his eyes.
NASB.12: I am writing to you, little children, because your sins have been forgiven you for His name's sake.
NASB.13: I am writing to you, fathers, because you know Him who has been from the beginning. I am writing to you, young men, because you have overcome the evil one. I have written to you, children, because you know the Father.
NASB.14: I have written to you, fathers, because you know Him who has been from the beginning. I have written to you, young men, because you are strong, and the word of God abides in you, and you have overcome the evil one.
NASB.15: Do not love the world nor the things in the world. If anyone loves the world, the love of the Father is not in him.
NASB.16: For all that is in the world, the lust of the flesh and the lust of the eyes and the boastful pride of life, is not from the Father, but is from the world.
NASB.17: The world is passing away, and [also] its lusts; but the one who does the will of God lives forever.
NASB.18: Children, it is the last hour; and just as you heard that antichrist is coming, even now many antichrists have appeared; from this we know that it is the last hour.
NASB.19: They went out from us, but they were not [really] of us; for if they had been of us, they would have remained with us; but [they went out], so that it would be shown that they all are not of us.
NASB.20: But you have an anointing from the Holy One, and you all know.
NASB.21: I have not written to you because you do not know the truth, but because you do know it, and because no lie is of the truth.
NASB.22: Who is the liar but the one who denies that Jesus is the Christ? This is the antichrist, the one who denies the Father and the Son.
NASB.23: Whoever denies the Son does not have the Father; the one who confesses the Son has the Father also.
NASB.24: As for you, let that abide in you which you heard from the beginning. If what you heard from the beginning abides in you, you also will abide in the Son and in the Father.
NASB.25: This is the promise which He Himself made to us: eternal life.
NASB.26: These things I have written to you concerning those who are trying to deceive you.
NASB.27: As for you, the anointing which you received from Him abides in you, and you have no need for anyone to teach you; but as His anointing teaches you about all things, and is true and is not a lie, and just as it has taught you, you abide in Him.
NASB.28: Now, little children, abide in Him, so that when He appears, we may have confidence and not shrink away from Him in shame at His coming.
NASB.29: If you know that He is righteous, you know that everyone also who practices righteousness is born of Him.

NLT.1: My dear children, I am writing this to you so that you will not sin. But if anyone does sin, we have an advocate who pleads our case before the Father. He is Jesus Christ, the one who is truly righteous.
NLT.2: He himself is the sacrifice that atones for our sins — and not only our sins but the sins of all the world.
NLT.3: And we can be sure that we know him if we obey his commandments.
NLT.4: If someone claims, “I know God,” but doesn’t obey God’s commandments, that person is a liar and is not living in the truth.
NLT.5: But those who obey God’s word truly show how completely they love him. That is how we know we are living in him.
NLT.6: Those who say they live in God should live their lives as Jesus did.
NLT.7: Dear friends, I am not writing a new commandment for you; rather it is an old one you have had from the very beginning. This old commandment — to love one another — is the same message you heard before.
NLT.8: Yet it is also new. Jesus lived the truth of this commandment, and you also are living it. For the darkness is disappearing, and the true light is already shining.
NLT.9: If anyone claims, “I am living in the light,” but hates a fellow believer, that person is still living in darkness.
NLT.10: Anyone who loves a fellow believer is living in the light and does not cause others to stumble.
NLT.11: But anyone who hates a fellow believer is still living and walking in darkness. Such a person does not know the way to go, having been blinded by the darkness.
NLT.12:
I am writing to you who are God’s children
because your sins have been forgiven through Jesus.
NLT.13:
I am writing to you who are mature in the faith
because you know Christ, who existed from the beginning.
I am writing to you who are young in the faith
because you have won your battle with the evil one.
NLT.14:
I have written to you who are God’s children
because you know the Father.
I have written to you who are mature in the faith
because you know Christ, who existed from the beginning.
I have written to you who are young in the faith
because you are strong.
God’s word lives in your hearts,
and you have won your battle with the evil one.
NLT.15: Do not love this world nor the things it offers you, for when you love the world, you do not have the love of the Father in you.
NLT.16: For the world offers only a craving for physical pleasure, a craving for everything we see, and pride in our achievements and possessions. These are not from the Father, but are from this world.
NLT.17: And this world is fading away, along with everything that people crave. But anyone who does what pleases God will live forever.
NLT.18: Dear children, the last hour is here. You have heard that the Antichrist is coming, and already many such antichrists have appeared. From this we know that the last hour has come.
NLT.19: These people left our churches, but they never really belonged with us; otherwise they would have stayed with us. When they left, it proved that they did not belong with us.
NLT.20: But you are not like that, for the Holy One has given you his Spirit, and all of you know the truth.
NLT.21: So I am writing to you not because you don’t know the truth but because you know the difference between truth and lies.
NLT.22: And who is a liar? Anyone who says that Jesus is not the Christ. Anyone who denies the Father and the Son is an antichrist.
NLT.23: Anyone who denies the Son doesn’t have the Father, either. But anyone who acknowledges the Son has the Father also.
NLT.24: So you must remain faithful to what you have been taught from the beginning. If you do, you will remain in fellowship with the Son and with the Father.
NLT.25: And in this fellowship we enjoy the eternal life he promised us.
NLT.26: I am writing these things to warn you about those who want to lead you astray.
NLT.27: But you have received the Holy Spirit, and he lives within you, so you don’t need anyone to teach you what is true. For the Spirit teaches you everything you need to know, and what he teaches is true — it is not a lie. So just as he has taught you, remain in fellowship with Christ.
NLT.28: And now, dear children, remain in fellowship with Christ so that when he returns, you will be full of courage and not shrink back from him in shame.
NLT.29: Since we know that Christ is righteous, we also know that all who do what is right are God’s children.

WEB.1: My little children, I write these things to you so that you may not sin. If anyone sins, we have a Counselor  with the Father, Jesus Christ, the righteous.
WEB.2: And he is the atoning sacrifice  for our sins, and not for ours only, but also for the whole world.
WEB.3: This is how we know that we know him: if we keep his commandments.
WEB.4: One who says, “I know him,” and doesn’t keep his commandments, is a liar, and the truth isn’t in him.
WEB.5: But whoever keeps his word, God’s love has most certainly been perfected in him. This is how we know that we are in him:
WEB.6: he who says he remains in him ought himself also to walk just like he walked.
WEB.7: Brothers, I write no new commandment to you, but an old commandment which you had from the beginning. The old commandment is the word which you heard from the beginning.
WEB.8: Again, I write a new commandment to you, which is true in him and in you; because the darkness is passing away, and the true light already shines.
WEB.9: He who says he is in the light and hates his brother, is in the darkness even until now.
WEB.10: He who loves his brother remains in the light, and there is no occasion for stumbling in him.
WEB.11: But he who hates his brother is in the darkness, and walks in the darkness, and doesn’t know where he is going, because the darkness has blinded his eyes.
WEB.12: I write to you, little children, because your sins are forgiven you for his name’s sake.
WEB.13: I write to you, fathers, because you know him who is from the beginning. I write to you, young men, because you have overcome the evil one. I write to you, little children, because you know the Father.
WEB.14: I have written to you, fathers, because you know him who is from the beginning. I have written to you, young men, because you are strong, and the word of God remains in you, and you have overcome the evil one.
WEB.15: Don’t love the world or the things that are in the world. If anyone loves the world, the Father’s love isn’t in him.
WEB.16: For all that is in the world, the lust of the flesh, the lust of the eyes, and the pride of life, isn’t the Father’s, but is the world’s.
WEB.17: The world is passing away with its lusts, but he who does God’s will remains forever.
WEB.18: Little children, these are the end times, and as you heard that the Antichrist is coming, even now many antichrists have arisen. By this we know that it is the final hour.
WEB.19: They went out from us, but they didn’t belong to us; for if they had belonged to us, they would have continued with us. But they left, that they might be revealed that none of them belong to us.
WEB.20: You have an anointing from the Holy One, and you all have knowledge.
WEB.21: I have not written to you because you don’t know the truth, but because you know it, and because no lie is of the truth.
WEB.22: Who is the liar but he who denies that Jesus is the Christ? This is the Antichrist, he who denies the Father and the Son.
WEB.23: Whoever denies the Son, the same doesn’t have the Father. He who confesses the Son has the Father also.
WEB.24: Therefore, as for you, let that remain in you which you heard from the beginning. If that which you heard from the beginning remains in you, you also will remain in the Son, and in the Father.
WEB.25: This is the promise which he promised us, the eternal life.
WEB.26: These things I have written to you concerning those who would lead you astray.
WEB.27: As for you, the anointing which you received from him remains in you, and you don’t need for anyone to teach you. But as his anointing teaches you concerning all things, and is true, and is no lie, and even as it taught you, you will remain in him.
WEB.28: Now, little children, remain in him, that when he appears, we may have boldness, and not be ashamed before him at his coming.
WEB.29: If you know that he is righteous, you know that everyone who practices righteousness has been born of him.

CSB17.1: My little children, I am writing you these things so that you may not sin. But if anyone does sin, we have an advocate <sup>ⓔ</sup> with the Father — Jesus Christ the righteous one.
CSB17.2: He himself is the atoning sacrifice <sup>[1]</sup> <sup>ⓕ</sup> for our sins, and not only for ours, but also for those of the whole world. <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.3: This is how we know <sup>ⓗ</sup> that we know him: if we keep his commands. <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.4: The one who says, “I have come to know him,” and yet doesn’t keep his commands, is a liar, and the truth is not in him.
CSB17.5: But whoever keeps his word, <sup>ⓙ</sup> truly in him the love of God is made complete. This is how we know we are in him: <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.6: The one who says he remains in him <sup>ⓛ</sup> should walk just as he walked.
CSB17.7: Dear friends, I am not writing you a new command <sup>ⓜ</sup> but an old command that you have had from the beginning. The old command is the word <sup>ⓝ</sup> you have heard.
CSB17.8: Yet I am writing you a new command, which is true in him and in you, because the darkness is passing away <sup>ⓐ</sup> and the true light <sup>ⓑ</sup> is already shining. <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.9: The one who says he is in the light but hates his brother or sister <sup>ⓓ</sup> is in the darkness until now.
CSB17.10: The one who loves his brother or sister <sup>ⓔ</sup> remains in the light, and there is no cause for stumbling in him. <sup>[2]</sup>
CSB17.11: But the one who hates his brother or sister <sup>ⓕ</sup> is in the darkness, walks in the darkness, <sup>ⓖ</sup> and doesn’t know where he’s going, <sup>ⓗ</sup> because the darkness has blinded his eyes.
CSB17.12:
I am writing to you, little children,
since your sins have been forgiven
on account of his name. ⓘ
CSB17.13:
I am writing to you, fathers,
because you have come to know
the one who is from the beginning. ⓙ
I am writing to you, young men,
because you have conquered the evil one. ⓚ
CSB17.14:
I have written to you, children,
because you have come to know the Father.
I have written to you, fathers,
because you have come to know
the one who is from the beginning.
I have written to you, young men,
because you are strong,
God’s word ⓛ remains in you,
and you have conquered the evil one.
CSB17.15: Do not love the world <sup>ⓜ</sup> or the things in the world. If anyone loves the world, the love of the Father is not in him.
CSB17.16: For everything in the world — the lust of the flesh, <sup>ⓝ</sup> the lust of the eyes, <sup>ⓐ</sup> and the pride <sup>ⓑ</sup> in one’s possessions — is not from the Father, but is from the world.
CSB17.17: And the world with its lust is passing away, <sup>ⓒ</sup> but the one who does the will of God <sup>ⓓ</sup> remains forever. <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.18: Children, it is the last hour. <sup>ⓕ</sup> And as you have heard that antichrist <sup>ⓖ</sup> is coming, even now many antichrists have come. By this we know that it is the last hour.
CSB17.19: They went out from us, but they did not belong to us; for if they had belonged to us, they would have remained with us. However, they went out so that it might be made clear that none of them belongs to us.
CSB17.20: But you have an anointing <sup>ⓗ</sup> from the Holy One, <sup>ⓘ</sup> and all of you know the truth. <sup>[3]</sup>
CSB17.21: I have not written to you because you don’t know the truth, but because you do know it, and because no lie comes from the truth.
CSB17.22: Who is the liar, if not the one who denies that Jesus is the Christ? <sup>ⓙ</sup> This one is the antichrist: the one who denies the Father and the Son.
CSB17.23: No one who denies the Son has the Father; <sup>ⓚ</sup> he who confesses the Son has the Father as well. <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.24: What you have heard from the beginning is to remain in you. If what you have heard from the beginning remains in you, then you will remain in the Son <sup>ⓜ</sup> and in the Father. <sup>ⓝ</sup>
CSB17.25: And this is the promise that he himself made to us: eternal life. <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.26: I have written these things to you concerning those who are trying to deceive you.
CSB17.27: As for you, the anointing you received from him remains in you, and you don’t need anyone to teach you. Instead, his anointing teaches you about all things <sup>ⓑ</sup> and is true and is not a lie; just as it has taught you, <sup>[4]</sup> remain in him. <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.28: So now, little children, remain in him so that when he appears <sup>ⓓ</sup> we may have confidence and not be ashamed before him at his coming. <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.29: If you know that he is righteous, you know this as well: Everyone who does what is right <sup>ⓕ</sup> has been born of him. <sup>ⓖ</sup>

AMP.1: My little children (believers, dear ones), I am writing you these things so that you will not sin and violate God's law. And if anyone sins, we have an Advocate [who will intercede for us] with the Father: Jesus Christ the righteous [the upright, the just One, who conforms to the Father's will in every way—purpose, thought, and action].
AMP.2: And He [that same Jesus] is the propitiation for our sins [the atoning sacrifice that holds back the wrath of God that would otherwise be directed at us because of our sinful nature—our worldliness, our lifestyle]; and not for ours alone, but also for [the sins of all believers throughout] the whole world.
AMP.3: And this is how we know [daily, by experience] that we have come to know Him [to understand Him and be more deeply acquainted with Him]: if we habitually keep [focused on His precepts and obey] His commandments (teachings).
AMP.4: Whoever says, "I have come to know Him," but does not habitually keep [focused on His precepts and obey] His commandments (teachings), is a liar, and the truth [of the divine word] is not in him.
AMP.5: But whoever habitually keeps His word and obeys His precepts [and treasures His message in its entirety], in him the love of God has truly been perfected [it is completed and has reached maturity]. By this we know [for certain] that we are in Him:
AMP.6: whoever says he lives in Christ [that is, whoever says he has accepted Him as God and Savior] ought [as a moral obligation] to walk and conduct himself just as He walked and conducted Himself.
AMP.7: Beloved, I am not writing a new commandment to you, but an old commandment which you have had from the beginning; the old commandment is the message which you have heard [before from us].
AMP.8: On the other hand, I am writing a new commandment to you, which is true and realized in Christ and in you, because the darkness [of moral blindness] is clearing away and the true Light [the revelation of God in Christ] is already shining.
AMP.9: The one who says he is in the Light [in consistent fellowship with Christ] and yethabitually hates (works against) his brother [in Christ] is in the darkness until now.
AMP.10: The one who loves and unselfishly seeks the best for his [believing] brother lives in the Light, and in him there is no occasion for stumbling or offense [he does not hurt the cause of Christ or lead others to sin].
AMP.11: But the one who habitually hates (works against) his brother [in Christ] is in [spiritual] darkness and is walking in the darkness, and does not know where he is going because the darkness has blinded his eyes.
AMP.12: I am writing to you, little children (believers, dear ones), because your sins have been forgiven for His name's sake [you have been pardoned and released from spiritual debt through His name because you have confessed His name, believing in Him as Savior].
AMP.13: I am writing to you, fathers [those believers who are spiritually mature], because you know Him who has existed from the beginning. I am writing to you, young men [those believers who are growing in spiritual maturity], because you have been victorious and have overcome the evil one. I have written to you, children [those who are new believers, those spiritually immature], because you have come to know the Father.
AMP.14: I have written to you, fathers, because you know Him who has existed from the beginning. I have written to you, young men, because you are strong and vigorous, and the word of God remains [always] in you, and you have been victorious over the evil one [by accepting Jesus as Savior].
AMP.15: Do not love the world [of sin that opposes God and His precepts], nor the things that are in the world. If anyone loves the world, the love of the Father is not in him.
AMP.16: For all that is in the world—the lust and sensual craving of the flesh and the lust and longing of the eyes and the boastful pride of life [pretentious confidence in one's resources or in the stability of earthly things]—these do not come from the Father, but are from the world.
AMP.17: The world is passing away, and with it its lusts [the shameful pursuits and ungodly longings]; but the one who does the will of God and carries out His purposes lives forever.
AMP.18: Children, it is the last hour [the end of this age]; and just as you heard that the antichrist is coming [the one who will oppose Christ and attempt to replace Him], even now many antichrists (false teachers) have appeared, which confirms our belief that it is the last hour.
AMP.19: They went out from us [seeming at first to be Christians], but they were not really of us [because they were not truly born again and spiritually transformed]; for if they had been of us, they would have remained with us; but they went out [teaching false doctrine], so that it would be clearly shown that none of them are of us.
AMP.20: But you have an anointing from the Holy One [you have been set apart, specially gifted and prepared by the Holy Spirit], and all of you know [the truth because He teaches us, illuminates our minds, and guards us from error].
AMP.21: I have not written to you because you do not know the truth, but because you do know it, and because no lie [nothing false, no deception] is of the truth.
AMP.22: Who is the liar but the one who denies that Jesus is the Christ (the Messiah, the Anointed)? This is the antichrist [the enemy and antagonist of Christ], the one who denies and consistently refuses to acknowledge the Father and the Son.
AMP.23: Whoever denies and repudiates the Son does not have the Father; the one who confesses and acknowledges the Son has the Father also.
AMP.24: As for you, let that remain in you [keeping in your hearts that message of salvation] which you heard from the beginning. If what you heard from the beginning remains in you, you too will remain in the Son and in the Father [forever].
AMP.25: This is the promise which He Himself promised us—eternal life.
AMP.26: These things I have written to you with reference to those who are trying to deceive you [seducing you and leading you away from the truth and sound doctrine].
AMP.27: As for you, the anointing [the special gift, the preparation] which you received from Him remains [permanently] in you, and you have no need for anyone to teach you. But just as His anointing teaches you [giving you insight through the presence of the Holy Spirit] about all things, and is true and is not a lie, and just as His anointing has taught you, you must remain in Him [being rooted in Him, knit to Him].
AMP.28: Now, little children (believers, dear ones), remain in Him [with unwavering faith], so that when He appears [at His return], we may have [perfect] confidence and not be ashamed and shrink away from Him at His coming.
AMP.29: If you know that He is absolutely righteous, you know [for certain] that everyone who practices righteousness [doing what is right and conforming to God's will] has been born of Him.

MSG.1: I write this, dear children, to guide you out of sin. But if anyone does sin, we have a Priest-Friend in the presence of the Father: Jesus Christ, righteous Jesus.
MSG.2: When he served as a sacrifice for our sins, he solved the sin problem for good—not only ours, but the whole world's.
MSG.3: Here's how we can be sure that we know God in the right way: Keep his commandments.
MSG.4: If someone claims, "I know him well!" but doesn't keep his commandments, he's obviously a liar. His life doesn't match his words.
MSG.5: But the one who keeps God's word is the person in whom we see God's mature love. This is the only way to be sure we're in God.
MSG.6: Anyone who claims to be intimate with God ought to live the same kind of life Jesus lived.
MSG.7: My dear friends, I'm not writing anything new here. This is the oldest commandment in the book, and you've known it from day one. It's always been implicit in the Message you've heard.
MSG.8: On the other hand, perhaps it is new, freshly minted as it is in both Christ and you—the darkness on its way out and the True Light already blazing!
MSG.9: Anyone who claims to live in God's light and hates a brother or sister is still in the dark.
MSG.10: It's the person who loves brother and sister who dwells in God's light and doesn't block the light from others.
MSG.11: But whoever hates is still in the dark, stumbles around in the dark, doesn't know which end is up, blinded by the darkness.
MSG.12: I remind you, my dear children: Your sins are forgiven in Jesus' name.
MSG.13: You veterans were in on the ground floor, and know the One who started all this; you newcomers have won a big victory over the Evil One. And a second reminder, dear children: You know the Father from personal experience.
MSG.14: You veterans know the One who started it all; and you newcomers—such vitality and strength! God's word is so steady in you. Your fellowship with God enables you to gain a victory over the Evil One.
MSG.15: Don't love the world's ways. Don't love the world's goods. Love of the world squeezes out love for the Father.
MSG.16: Practically everything that goes on in the world—wanting your own way, wanting everything for yourself, wanting to appear important—has nothing to do with the Father. It just isolates you from him.
MSG.17: The world and all its wanting, wanting, wanting is on the way out—but whoever does what God wants is set for eternity.
MSG.18: Children, time is just about up. You heard that Antichrist is coming. Well, they're all over the place, antichrists everywhere you look. That's how we know that we're close to the end.
MSG.19: They left us, but they were never really with us. If they had been, they would have stuck it out with us, loyal to the end. In leaving, they showed their true colors, showed they never did belong.
MSG.20: But you belong. The Holy One anointed you, and you all know it.
MSG.21: I haven't been writing this to tell you something you don't know, but to confirm the truth you do know, and to remind you that the truth doesn't breed lies.
MSG.22: So who is lying here? It's the person who denies that Jesus is the Divine Christ, that's who. This is what makes an antichrist: denying the Father, denying the Son.
MSG.23: No one who denies the Son has any part with the Father, but affirming the Son is an embrace of the Father as well.
MSG.24: Stay with what you heard from the beginning, the original message. Let it sink into your life. If what you heard from the beginning lives deeply in you, you will live deeply in both Son and Father.
MSG.25: This is exactly what Christ promised: eternal life, real life!
MSG.26: I've written to warn you about those who are trying to deceive you.
MSG.27: But they're no match for what is embedded deeply within you—Christ's anointing, no less! You don't need any of their so-called teaching. Christ's anointing teaches you the truth on everything you need to know about yourself and him, uncontaminated by a single lie. Live deeply in what you were taught.
MSG.28: And now, children, stay with Christ. Live deeply in Christ. Then we'll be ready for him when he appears, ready to receive him with open arms, with no cause for red-faced guilt or lame excuses when he arrives.
MSG.29: Once you're convinced that he is right and righteous, you'll recognize that all who practice righteousness are God's true children.

NIV.1: My dear children, I write this to you so that you will not sin. But if anybody does sin, we have one who speaks to the Father in our defense—Jesus Christ, the Righteous One.
NIV.2: He is the atoning sacrifice for our sins, and not only for ours but also for the sins of the whole world.
NIV.3: We know that we have come to know him if we obey his commands.
NIV.4: The man who says, “I know him,” but does not do what he commands is a liar, and the truth is not in him.
NIV.5: But if anyone obeys his word, God’s love is truly made complete in him. This is how we know we are in him:
NIV.6: Whoever claims to live in him must walk as Jesus did.
NIV.7: Dear friends, I am not writing you a new command but an old one, which you have had since the beginning. This old command is the message you have heard.
NIV.8: Yet I am writing you a new command; its truth is seen in him and you, because the darkness is passing and the true light is already shining.
NIV.9: Anyone who claims to be in the light but hates his brother is still in the darkness.
NIV.10: Whoever loves his brother lives in the light, and there is nothing in him to make him stumble.
NIV.11: But whoever hates his brother is in the darkness and walks around in the darkness; he does not know where he is going, because the darkness has blinded him.
NIV.12:
I write to you, dear children,
because your sins have been forgiven on account of his name.
NIV.13:
I write to you, fathers,
because you have known him who is from the beginning.
I write to you, young men,
because you have overcome the evil one.
I write to you, dear children,
because you have known the Father.
NIV.14:
I write to you, fathers,
because you have known him who is from the beginning.
I write to you, young men,
because you are strong,
and the word of God lives in you,
and you have overcome the evil one.
NIV.15:
Do Not Love the World
Do not love the world or anything in the world. If anyone loves the world, the love of the Father is not in him.
NIV.16: For everything in the world—the cravings of sinful man, the lust of his eyes and the boasting of what he has and does—comes not from the Father but from the world.
NIV.17: The world and its desires pass away, but the man who does the will of God lives forever.
NIV.18:
Warning Against Antichrists
Dear children, this is the last hour; and as you have heard that the antichrist is coming, even now many antichrists have come. This is how we know it is the last hour.
NIV.19: They went out from us, but they did not really belong to us. For if they had belonged to us, they would have remained with us; but their going showed that none of them belonged to us.
NIV.20: But you have an anointing from the Holy One, and all of you know the truth.
NIV.21: I do not write to you because you do not know the truth, but because you do know it and because no lie comes from the truth.
NIV.22: Who is the liar? It is the man who denies that Jesus is the Christ. Such a man is the antichrist—he denies the Father and the Son.
NIV.23: No one who denies the Son has the Father; whoever acknowledges the Son has the Father also.
NIV.24: See that what you have heard from the beginning remains in you. If it does, you also will remain in the Son and in the Father.
NIV.25: And this is what he promised us—even eternal life.
NIV.26: I am writing these things to you about those who are trying to lead you astray.
NIV.27: As for you, the anointing you received from him remains in you, and you do not need anyone to teach you. But as his anointing teaches you about all things and as that anointing is real, not counterfeit—just as it has taught you, remain in him.
NIV.28:
Children of God
And now, dear children, continue in him, so that when he appears we may be confident and unashamed before him at his coming.
NIV.29: If you know that he is righteous, you know that everyone who does what is right has been born of him.

NRSVCE.1: My little children, I am writing these things to you so that you may not sin. But if anyone does sin, we have an advocate with the Father, Jesus Christ the righteous;
NRSVCE.2: and he is the atoning sacrifice for our sins, and not for ours only but also for the sins of the whole world.
NRSVCE.3: Now by this we may be sure that we know him, if we obey his commandments.
NRSVCE.4: Whoever says, “I have come to know him,” but does not obey his commandments, is a liar, and in such a person the truth does not exist;
NRSVCE.5: but whoever obeys his word, truly in this person the love of God has reached perfection. By this we may be sure that we are in him:
NRSVCE.6: whoever says, “I abide in him,” ought to walk just as he walked.
NRSVCE.7: Beloved, I am writing you no new commandment, but an old commandment that you have had from the beginning; the old commandment is the word that you have heard.
NRSVCE.8: Yet I am writing you a new commandment that is true in him and in you, because the darkness is passing away and the true light is already shining.
NRSVCE.9: Whoever says, “I am in the light,” while hating a brother or sister, is still in the darkness.
NRSVCE.10: Whoever loves a brother or sister lives in the light, and in such a person there is no cause for stumbling.
NRSVCE.11: But whoever hates another believer is in the darkness, walks in the darkness, and does not know the way to go, because the darkness has brought on blindness.
NRSVCE.12:
I am writing to you, little children,
because your sins are forgiven on account of his name.
NRSVCE.13:
I am writing to you, fathers,
because you know him who is from the beginning.
I am writing to you, young people,
because you have conquered the evil one.
NRSVCE.14:
I write to you, children,
because you know the Father.
I write to you, fathers,
because you know him who is from the beginning.
I write to you, young people,
because you are strong
and the word of God abides in you,
and you have overcome the evil one.
NRSVCE.15: Do not love the world or the things in the world. The love of the Father is not in those who love the world;
NRSVCE.16: for all that is in the world — the desire of the flesh, the desire of the eyes, the pride in riches — comes not from the Father but from the world.
NRSVCE.17: And the world and its desire are passing away, but those who do the will of God live forever.
NRSVCE.18: Children, it is the last hour! As you have heard that antichrist is coming, so now many antichrists have come. From this we know that it is the last hour.
NRSVCE.19: They went out from us, but they did not belong to us; for if they had belonged to us, they would have remained with us. But by going out they made it plain that none of them belongs to us.
NRSVCE.20: But you have been anointed by the Holy One, and all of you have knowledge.
NRSVCE.21: I write to you, not because you do not know the truth, but because you know it, and you know that no lie comes from the truth.
NRSVCE.22: Who is the liar but the one who denies that Jesus is the Christ? This is the antichrist, the one who denies the Father and the Son.
NRSVCE.23: No one who denies the Son has the Father; everyone who confesses the Son has the Father also.
NRSVCE.24: Let what you heard from the beginning abide in you. If what you heard from the beginning abides in you, then you will abide in the Son and in the Father.
NRSVCE.25: And this is what he has promised us, eternal life.
NRSVCE.26: I write these things to you concerning those who would deceive you.
NRSVCE.27: As for you, the anointing that you received from him abides in you, and so you do not need anyone to teach you. But as his anointing teaches you about all things, and is true and is not a lie, and just as it has taught you, abide in him.
NRSVCE.28: And now, little children, abide in him, so that when he is revealed we may have confidence and not be put to shame before him at his coming.
NRSVCE.29: If you know that he is righteous, you may be sure that everyone who does right has been born of him.

NET.1: (My little children, I am writing these things to you so that you may not sin. ) But if anyone does sin, we have an advocate with the Father, Jesus Christ the righteous One,
NET.2: and he himself is the atoning sacrifice for our sins, and not only for our sins but also for the whole world.
NET.3: Now by this we know that we have come to know God: if we keep his commandments.
NET.4: The one who says “I have come to know God” and yet does not keep his commandments is a liar, and the truth is not in such a person.
NET.5: But whoever obeys his word, truly in this person the love of God has been perfected. By this we know that we are in him.
NET.6: The one who says he resides in God ought himself to walk just as Jesus walked.
NET.7: Dear friends, I am not writing a new commandment to you, but an old commandment which you have had from the beginning. The old commandment is the word that you have already heard.
NET.8: On the other hand, I am writing a new commandment to you which is true in him and in you, because the darkness is passing away and the true light is already shining.
NET.9: The one who says he is in the light but still hates his fellow Christian is still in the darkness.
NET.10: The one who loves his fellow Christian resides in the light, and there is no cause for stumbling in him.
NET.11: But the one who hates his fellow Christian is in the darkness, walks in the darkness, and does not know where he is going, because the darkness has blinded his eyes.
NET.12: I am writing to you, little children, that your sins have been forgiven because of his name.
NET.13: I am writing to you, fathers, that you have known him who has been from the beginning. I am writing to you, young people, that you have conquered the evil one.
NET.14: I have written to you, children, that you have known the Father. I have written to you, fathers, that you have known him who has been from the beginning. I have written to you, young people, that you are strong, and the word of God resides in you, and you have conquered the evil one.
NET.15: Do not love the world or the things in the world. If anyone loves the world, the love of the Father is not in him,
NET.16: because all that is in the world (the desire of the flesh and the desire of the eyes and the arrogance produced by material possessions) is not from the Father, but is from the world.
NET.17: And the world is passing away with all its desires, but the person who does the will of God remains forever.
NET.18: Children, it is the last hour, and just as you heard that the antichrist is coming, so now many antichrists have appeared. We know from this that it is the last hour.
NET.19: They went out from us, but they did not really belong to us, because if they had belonged to us, they would have remained with us. But they went out from us to demonstrate that all of them do not belong to us.
NET.20: Nevertheless you have an anointing from the Holy One, and you all know.
NET.21: I have not written to you that you do not know the truth, but that you do know it, and that no lie is of the truth.
NET.22: Who is the liar but the person who denies that Jesus is the Christ ? This one is the antichrist: the person who denies the Father and the Son.
NET.23: Everyone who denies the Son does not have the Father either. The person who confesses the Son has the Father also.
NET.24: As for you, what you have heard from the beginning must remain in you. If what you heard from the beginning remains in you, you also will remain in the Son and in the Father.
NET.25: Now this is the promise that he himself made to us: eternal life.
NET.26: These things I have written to you about those who are trying to deceive you.
NET.27: Now as for you, the anointing that you received from him resides in you, and you have no need for anyone to teach you. But as his anointing teaches you about all things, it is true and is not a lie. Just as it has taught you, you reside in him.
NET.28: And now, little children, remain in him, so that when he appears we may have confidence and not shrink away from him in shame when he comes back.
NET.29: If you know that he is righteous, you also know that everyone who practices righteousness has been fathered by him.
